NOTICE OF HEARING TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION'S HEARING TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS OFF CALENDAR PER REQUEST OF THE MOVING PARTY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 18, 2014, TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY A CHANCE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL 2.6B. THE OPPOSING PARTY SHALL IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSES AT THE HEARING THAT SHE KNOWS AND IS SOCIAL FRIENDS WITH DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY ELIZABETH SALVESON. JUDGE MILLER PRACTICED LAW IN THE SAME FIRM AS MS. SALVESON FROM DECEMBER 1981 - MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE, AND SHE DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD REASONABLY HAVE A DOUBT THAT SHE WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. RULING: PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976 (302)

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Mar-18-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Betz, Esq., 925.609.1699, for petitioner San Francisco Deputy Sheriff's Association.
Peter Saltzman, Esq., 510.272.0169, for respondent San Francisco Managers and Supervisors Association.
Terence Howzell, Deputy City Attorney, 510.554.3800, for respondent City and County of San Francisco.

Judge Miller discloses at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms. Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case, and she does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial.

RULING: Argued and the Court denied PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION'S Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Ntc Of Hearing To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Continued To Mar-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION's Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award
is continued to March 18, 2014, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)

Petitioner San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association'S Hearing To Vacate Arbitration Award

No appearances. The matter is off calendar per request of the moving party. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185 (302/MJM)
